t_ch: 至少能让人忘掉很多烦恼。
t_en: At the very least, it allows one to forget many a worry.

t_ch: 这世界上最厉害的股神，
t_en: The greatest stock market genius in the world,
is a fox who likes sitting at round tables.
A fox lives to make money!
...What's the point of making money?
Uh, keep on earning that dough!
Hey, we just love hearing that clink~

t_ch: 我笑那阿哈无谋，愚者少智。
t_en: I laugh at Aha's lack of cunning and the Fools' naïveté.
Holding onto the tavern at the end of the world like that...

t_ch: 不知道变成概念股去上市。
t_en: But who knows, maybe it'll become an Index Funds and go public.
Don't be afraid...
It's a technical correction...
Oh my gosh! I'm the Toilet Knight!
Fly, my glorious glutes!
Yeah! Yeah! Totally flying high, woo-hoo!
I'm gonna faaaall!!!
Let's bounce!
Three, two, one, jump!

t_ch: 快使用二段跳，嚯嚯哈嘿！
t_en: Quick, use your double jump! Hiya-hah-hey!

t_ch: 进入附近的<color=#f29e38ff>「二次元JUMP！」</color>寻找我吧！
t_en: Come find me at the nearby <color=#f29e38ff>Interplanar Jump</color>!

t_ch: 在次元壁上按「下」就能离开~
t_en: Press "Down" on the Dimensional Wall to leave~
Try flipping the music score upside down?

t_ch: 据说，愚者们会在黑屏的时候偷偷更换对面的房间
t_en: Rumor has it that the Fools swap out the room across from you when the screen goes dark
Head out and you'll be on the rooftop!
This way to the imagenesis classroom!
Head out and you'll find the art classroom~
Head back to the ball court from here?
So the restricted section is through here?

t_ch: 这里可以返回档案室呢。
t_en: We can head back to the archive room from here.
How about we head back to the hallway from here?
Exit ahead, back to the classroom~
Return to the comic classroom from here~
Exit up ahead, the music room~
Just head out and you'll be across the river!
Head out here, and you'll be back on the main street.
Head out, and you'll be right back at the food street.

t_ch: 这里有什么神秘的房间？
t_en: What kind of mysterious room is this?

t_ch: 出去就到共愿帮的对岸啦~
t_en: Once you head out, you'll be on the opposite side of the Synwish Syndicate~
That's the newspaper agency is right inside.
The office is just up ahead.

t_ch: 出去就能看到巨大的帝垣琼玉！
t_en: Just step outside and you'll see the massive Celestial Jade!
If you wanna jump to your heart's content, this way~
You can skate across the ice to the other side!
Head outside and you can get to the rooftop~

t_ch: 出去可以到更高的天台哦~
t_en: Head outside and you can get to the higher rooftop~
Heads up, you're about to enter the dark street...
Heads up! Once you leave, there's no coming back!
Head out, and you'll be back at the collection room!

t_ch: 前方就是黄金告解室…嗯嗯。
t_en: The Golden Confessional is just up ahead... Mhm.
Head across to get to the hospital.
Head across to get to the collection room.
This is... a training room?
Duck behind the wine rack!
Time to head up to the tavern's second floor!
Wanna get flushed?
Clinic ahead. Please keep quiet.
Target: Collection room, first floor!

t_ch: 回到桥上可以看看风景哦~
t_en: You can check out the view when you get back to the bridge~
What is this place?
We're inside the toy instrument!
Wanna play together?
Will there be more new friends...?
Let me tell you a joke...
Sorry, the dialogue box is too tiny
Guess how much I like you?
To the farthest star!
Come closer, let me get a whiff...
Don't worry... the spikes are made of gummy candy
Found me again, hehe
You're the Hide-and-Seek champion!
Wrong!
Nope~

t_ch: 终极挑战已开启，各单位警戒！
t_en: The Final Showdown commences! All units, constant vigilance!

t_ch: 随着宇宙中总熵的缓慢增加，短生物种将成为越来越普遍的现象：就像一年生植物比多年生植物更具演化优势一样。然而，长生是任何生命个体都会拥有的愿望——这就是银河中普遍的斗争。
t_en: With the slow increase in universal total entropy, it follows that short-lived species will become increasingly common, much as annual plants possess an evolutionary edge over perennials. However, the desire for longevity is innate to all life, giving rise to the struggle pervading the cosmos.

t_ch: 盛大的节日！人们最美丽的冲动都来自应对「无聊」。我们开拓新的世界，最重要的动机就是——我们不想再像过去的人们那样生活下去了！哈哈哈哈！
t_en: What a festival! Humanity's finest drives are born in the fight against "boredom." And our greatest reason to trailblaze new worlds is... our sheer refusal to live as those who came before us did! Hahahaha!

t_ch: 开拓并不意味着总要领头。领航员只有一个，无名客却可以有任意之多：我们这些跟随命运的人，未必就不如创造命运的人高贵。也就是说，粉丝或许比明星本身更有价值！
t_en: To trailblaze is not always to lead. There is only one Navigator, but there can be as many Nameless as the stars. Those who answer destiny's call are no less noble than those who issue it. Seen in this light, the fans might just be more valuable than the star themselves!

t_ch: 愿力可以是赢家，也可以是输家——当它是赢家的时候，它总是把事情变得更加简单；当它是输家的时候，它总是把事情变得更加复杂。
t_en: Wishpower can be a winner, or it can be a loser. As a winner, it always makes things easier. As a loser, it just complicates everything.

t_ch: 格斗游戏的招式可不能太少！在无名客的战斗里，一切都是武器，一切的战斗方式也都被允许——这才是真正肩负着宇宙生存的斗争！唯一的原则就是不能伤到同伴！
t_en: A fighting game can never have too many moves! In the combat of the Nameless, anything goes and everything's a weapon. That is the true struggle for cosmic survival! The only principle is: Do not hurt your companions!

t_ch: 啊，这该死的单向门。它让我想到我一直把某人当作朋友，可那人却一直把我当成敌人的往事…爱总是会宽恕被爱者，那人是这么说的——看来我并不热爱单向门！
t_en: Ugh, this cursed one-way door. It takes me back to when I once considered someone a friend, who in turn saw only an enemy... Love always forgives the beloved, or so they say. Well, it seems I feel no such charity for one-way doors!

t_ch: 下水道隐含了一个深刻的假设…关于熵的假设。按照这个假设，人类注定会一再衰败。他们唯一的救赎是获取食物——然后将它们献祭并用下水道冲走。下水道是文明最深刻的象征。
t_en: The sewer harbors a profound hypothesis... one of entropy. By it, humanity is doomed to recurrent decay. Our sole redemption is to procure sustenance, offer it up, and flush it away. Thus, the sewer stands as civilization's ultimate emblem.

t_ch: 神明的信仰者，往往假设神明不会对人类犯罪，人类的一切困境都是由他们自己可悲的能力造成。他们应该思考：万一神明的力量比人类还要可悲，那么他们会不会因为需要一个弱者而需要神明。
t_en: Believers operate on the assumption that gods do not sin against man, laying every human plight at the door of our own pathetic faculties. They really ought to reflect: if divine power proved even more paltry than that of humans, would they still need gods simply because they need an inferior?

t_ch: 人们总是为了利己和利他争论不休。但愿他们能够理解，这只是一种类似透视的作画技巧，不同的流派对它们有不同的应用。
t_en: People always exhaust themselves in the debate between egoism and altruism. Hopefully, they shall come to see that these are but principles of composition, like perspective, applied differently by different schools.

t_ch: 热爱和厌恶有时来源于同一种诱因。这种情况大多发生在有人把生牡蛎添加到汉堡当中的场合。
t_en: Love and hate sometimes share the same trigger. This situation mostly arises when someone decides to add raw oysters to a hamburger.

t_ch: 成为无名客吧，这是一种多么高贵的理想！但你也要想清楚…作为一块高贵的大理石，你真的认可这个理想，情愿它把你雕成不可改变的形状吗？友情提醒，雕刻比拍照可痛多了！
t_en: To be a Nameless is a noble ideal! But think this through... As a noble block of marble, do you truly accept this ideal? Are you willing to be shaped by it, irrevocably? Friendly reminder: The sculpting bites way deeper than any camera's flash!

t_ch: 我要尽情享用美食！不然我会无法忍受自己的弱点——那种弱点让我对着一切东西都要大吃特吃！我可不想在饥肠辘辘的时候面对那些吃完只会让我后悔的食物……
t_en: I'm going to indulge! Properly! If I don't, I can't bear this hunger so consuming that it makes me want to devour everything! On an empty stomach, I refuse any tempting food that I know will just leave me with regret...

t_ch: 漫画的重点，不仅在于让人理解，也在于让人「不理解」。「不理解」能够保护作品的主题不被随意践踏。「不理解」能够在之后理解的一瞬间释放巨大的能量。我要找到一本我不理解的漫画。
t_en: The point of comic isn't just getting people to understand, it's also getting them to "not understand." That "lack of understanding" protects the themes from being trampled on. And it is in the moment understanding breaks through that its power is fully unleashed. I must find a comic that I cannot understand.

t_ch: 一个自诩为天才的无家可归者。他因为极度的自我厌恶而对自己的精神发起了凶恶的复仇——并使得自己成为了「世界本无意义」的最佳注脚。讽刺地是，很少有人把他当作一个背叛者。
t_en: A self-proclaimed genius with no home to return to. In his extreme self-loathing, he waged a savage war upon his own psyche, rendering himself the perfect footnote to the claim that "the world is meaningless." Ironically, scarcely anyone ever saw him as a traitor.

t_ch: 不要模仿我的行为，否则你会被高速行驶的列车产生的吸力吞噬。我不怕吞噬，是因为我已经碎裂——而你还需要维持身体的健全。
t_en: Do not mimic my deeds, lest you be devoured by the suction generated by the high-speed Express. I do not fear being devoured, for I am already shattered. You, however, still owe wholeness to your form.

t_ch: 星神为何而存在？有人说，要是没有智慧的人，星神也就不会存在…某种意义上这的确言之有理。但是，这件事还有更冷酷的一面——要是没有愚蠢的人，星神就更不可能存在！
t_en: Why do the Aeons exist? Some say if there were no intelligent beings, the Aeons probably would not be... There's truth in that. But the colder logic runs deeper: without folly, the Aeons most certainly would not be!

t_ch: 欢迎光临！您是悲观主义者吗？咖啡会是您给自己的最好奖励。您是乐观主义者吗？咖啡也会是您给自己的最好奖励！
t_en: Welcome! Are you a pessimist? Then a perfect coffee is your best reward. An optimist? Then you need coffee all the more!

t_ch: 来喝汽水吧！这样动物们就不会把我们当作同类。不然，作为同类，它们会觉得我们又哭又笑，荒唐透顶，充满不幸…可不能让它们这么认为！
t_en: Let's drink some soda! So the animals won't mistake us for their own kind. Otherwise, seeing us cry and laugh, they'd think us such tragic, ridiculous creatures... And we mustn't let that happen!

t_ch: 「朋友们鼓掌吧，喜剧就要结束了！」…高傲的自封为神者以此自诩。冲吧，撕破他的面具，告诉他这个世界的观众究竟在期待什么！
t_en: "Applaud, friends, the comedy is over!" Thus crows the self-proclaimed god. Charge! Tear off his mask and tell him what this world's audience truly craves!

t_ch: 安全出口？无名客的世界里可没有那样的东西。选择其一，其他的可能性就会一并消失——关键的是，没有人能向你保证一个正确答案。
t_en: An emergency exit? Such a concept does not exist for the Nameless. To choose is to let a universe of alternatives die... And the kicker is, no one holds that guarantee of a correct answer.

t_ch: 什么叫公司呢？就是一种把幸福和不幸都当作电脑病毒一并清除的组织。
t_en: What is a corporation? It is an organization that deletes both happiness and misery alike as if they were system errors.

t_ch: 加速！加速！！加速！！！一切金钱的问题都可以用加速来解决——我说的。
t_en: Faster! Faster!! FASTER!!! Every money problem can be solved with more speed. Mark my word.

t_ch: 枣！尚！！嚎！！！当一个尘灵独处时，它说话会变得大声，写字会变得大声——它要制造一切行为的回声，好让天地万物都知道自己还存在。这是尘灵的美德。
t_en: Goooooood! Moooooooorning!!! When a wispae is alone, it speaks loudly and writes loudly to proclaim to the world, through echoes born of every act, that it still exists. That is the virtue of wispae.

t_ch: 人类？幻造种？尘灵？都不是，这年头嗓门最大的东西叫发电机。
t_en: Humans? Imagenae? Wispae? None of the above. These days, the loudest things around are called: generators.

t_ch: 呀吼！终于找到我了吗？我刚创作完一部悲剧，现在满脑子只想创作喜剧啦！哈哈哈哈。
t_en: Yahoo! Took you long enough! I just finished writing a tragedy, and now my mind is flooding with nothing but comedy! Hahahaha.

t_ch: 不用担心，我不会被一张假嘴吃掉——就算它是真的，尘灵也不够它塞牙缝啦。说真的，星神要想成为爱的对象，祂就必须先放弃审判的权威…嗯，我后面的这位老兄说不定还挺有前途。
t_en: Fear not, I'm in no danger from a literal fake mouth. And even if it's real, a wispae wouldn't be enough to barely count as a snack. Seriously though, if an Aeon wants to be a subject of love, THEY must first relinquish THEIR authority of judgment... Hmm, this buddy behind me might actually have prospects on that.

t_ch: 科幻片的敌人往往有一种共性——他们想要揭示这个世界是丑恶的，结果却把这个世界变得更丑恶了。
t_en: Sci-fi villains often fall for the same pattern: in their very attempt to expose the world's ugliness, they only succeed in adding to it.

t_ch: 嗯？伪装被识破了？太好了，总算有人帮我解套了。被人遗忘可比被人否定要难受多了。被人否定的时候起码还能发起战斗。
t_en: Hmm? I'm exposed? Wonderful! Finally, someone's here to get me off the hook. To be forgotten is a deeper wound than to be opposed. Opposition, at least, grants you a battlefield.

t_ch: 向死而生是一切生命的本质。哪怕长生种也会死于意外——在无限漫长的时间里，这件事会发生的概率是<unbreak>100%</unbreak>。是的：从长期主义的视角来看，我们都死了。
t_en: All existence is a march toward death. Even long-life species can die from accidents. Given infinite time, the probability of this happening is <unbreak>100%</unbreak>. Thus, from a long-term view, we are all dead.

t_ch: 追求真理的冲动，来源于保存生命的权力。这就是为什么，巨大的机器人就是正义！来吧，我们白刃格斗！
t_en: The impulse to seek truth springs from the will to preserve life. That's why giant robots equal justice! So draw steel and fight!

t_ch: 垃圾只是放错了位置的资源。
t_en: Trash is just a resource that hasn't found its place yet.

t_ch: 但我不是为了这个掏垃圾桶。
t_en: But that's not why I'm rummaging through trashcans.
I am searching for the ultimate art...
...aka my life.
Bunnies don't worry about a thing!

t_ch: 骗你的，它只是不说而已。
t_en: Just kidding. It does. It just keeps it all to itself.
It started out as one headache...
Speak up, and that makes two.
So why not just choose to be happy?
Wolfie, wolfie!

t_ch: 究竟更像狼，还是更像狗？
t_en: Is it more like a wolf, or a dog?
A wolf to foes.
A dog to friends.
So, stop acting so serious!

t_ch: 银河间有很多神奇的传说。
t_en: The cosmos is full of wondrous legends.
Like a chivalrous rabbit,
an express traversing the galaxy.

t_ch: 仔细想想全都荒谬至极。
t_en: Come to think of it, it's all sheer absurdity.

t_ch: 但，传说不就是因为荒谬，
t_en: But then, isn't absurdity the very thing...
that makes a legend?
When you clean,
remember to protect

t_ch: 夹在旧书中的一片叶子。
t_en: that one leaf tucked away in an old book.

t_ch: 有一次动物们想打棒球，
t_en: Once, the animals wanted to play baseball.
Unable to find a ball,
they just popped a bead off
the Divine Dragon's weapon.
Of course, they did put it back.
Acting serious is exhausting.
Video games are way more fun!
If you don't like taking photos,
It's probably just that you're missing—
a great makeup artist!
Silence is golden.
Which is to say,
the less you talk, the more money you save.
I guess...
Attention, passengers.
Remember to wash your face,
brush your teeth,
and give your feet a good clean before bed.
A nice hot bath would be perfect!
Don't just pass out, okay?
First things when you get home:
Shoes off!
Hands washed!
Clothes changed!
Good hygiene is a virtue~
Dare you hit a home run?
Just kidding.
I'm not build for that.
Friendship! Reality! Effort! Victory!
Victory never comes easy.
So take your rewards!
This is the life of every mechatron:
I'll be the head!

t_ch: 但如果身体也有自己的意识，
t_en: But if the body had a consciousness of its own,
it'd definitely say—
Why should you get to be the head??
This armor looks awesome!
The subtext here is...

t_ch: 生物都习惯用肩膀的宽度，
t_en: Creatures are used to judge body's strength,
by the width of the shoulders.

t_ch: 所以，人类的三角肌很重要！
t_en: That's why the deltoids are so crucial for humans!

t_ch: 机铠的脚可不仅仅是装饰。
t_en: The feet of mechatron aren't just for decoration.
For any sentient being,

t_ch: 诗和远方的前提都是脚。
t_en: Feet are the prerequisite for dreams and distant horizons.

t_ch: 承重的部件懂得脚踏实地。
t_en: A load-bearing part knows to keep its feet on the ground.
A load-bearing design endures in solitude.

t_ch: 恭喜你，站在了巨人的肩上！
t_en: Congrats, you're standing on the shoulders of giants!
Next up,

t_ch: 就是发挥想象力的时间了……
t_en: it's time to use your imagination...
In other words...

t_ch: 这里已经没什么值得留恋！
t_en: There's nothing left for you here!

t_ch: 勇敢地开拓未来吧，少年！
t_en: Brave soul, trailblaze the future!
Amidst the Quantum fluctuations,
can a lonely mechatron mind
also be born?
Clothes make the man,
clothes make the gods.
When looking for a job,
remember to suit up.
A journey of a thousand miles
begins with a single foot.
That is to say,
Mechatrons without feet are just couch potatoes!
Zandar also liked to say that

t_ch: 他只是站在巨人的肩上。
t_en: he was just standing on the shoulders of giants.
But perhaps that was meant to be ironic.
You know, Nous is but a giant head.
Congratulations on reaching the summit!
Hail the King of Mechs!
Uh, King or whatever, it doesn't matter.
Just come and take these rewards!
Hey! Over here!
Cheeeeese—
What?

t_ch: 你的球技竟然在我之上？
t_en: Wait, you're better than me at this?
A long dream is but a vision, fading into light tales.
Passenger...

t_ch: 你的房间多久没打扫了？
t_en: When was the last time you cleaned your room?
Body! A body!

t_ch: 我愿意用我的整个王国，
t_en: I'd trade my whole kingdom
for a body!
Final 3 days! Final 3 days!
Shoulder components, starting at <unbreak>90%</unbreak> off!
I am no decoration...

t_ch: 你们小人物是不会懂的。
t_en: You nobodies would never understand.
Pull the lever!
Have fun!
Congratulations!
Finally!
Go, hero!
It's moving!
Where are you taking me?
Now where was I headed again?
I'm all for this!
Aaallll for this!
All together now
The button is on my head!

t_ch: 和我一起逃离无聊的日常吧～
t_en: Let's escape from the drab daily life with me~

t_ch: 切换到黄色三角，再拨动拉杆～
t_en: Switch to the yellow triangle, then pull the lever~
Charge, charge, chaaarge!
Keep it going! Don't stop!
Let's send this place to the moon!
Time it right, pull the lever
Thank you, Trailblazemon!
Be careful not to drown
Next stop, Penacony!
Nice work!
Nice! Nice! Niiice!
Go!

t_ch: 是开拓妖精，我们有救了
t_en: It's Trailblazemon. We're saved!
Next stop, the Xianzhou Luofu!
Rending my form, setting free.
A thunderous roar, a symphony.
Soul's depths stirred by tempest's might,
Surging waves, an awe-inspiring sight.
Hot blood boiling, chest ablaze,
Fangs and claws, burnished glaze.
Adorned in starlight, I shall reascend.
Above the watery abyss, transcendent.
Welcome back to your post, high elder!
Please part the seas!
Punch, punch, fight!

t_ch: 胜败乃兵家常事，再来一次吧
t_en: Winning and losing are all part of the game, let's do it one more time
Tick-tock, please help us
An encouraging grunt.
The dream... is over, tweet~
Honestly, I can fly...
But I'm afraid of heights
Snap! I got the pic!
Nice Smile
Just hold "Left" and Sprint all the way!
Go!
You can do it!
That was a close call!
That's horrifying!
Run for your lives!
Spin! Jump!

t_ch: 你发出了一声不那么酷的*哼*，眼前一片漆黑……
t_en: You hmph in a not-so-cool way, and your surroundings turn pitch-black...

t_ch: 你输给了石头老板的爪牙，但不要憎恨你的敌人……
t_en: Don't hate the enemies that have beaten you, as they are merely Boss Stone's minions...

t_ch: 钟表小子和折纸小鸟可以粗心，但哈努兄弟不行……
t_en: Clockie and the Origami Bird can be careless, but not Hanu...

t_ch: 痛苦和恐惧不是失败，哈努兄弟还有挽回的余地……
t_en: Being in agony and fear doesn't mean he's a loser. Hanu can still salvage the situation...

t_ch: 哈努兄弟走错过路，但时间和运气会改正错误……
t_en: Hanu has made mistakes, but time and fate will correct them...

t_ch: 这里是梦境游戏《小小哈努行动》的入口，只有变成小小哈努兄弟后才可以进入。
t_en: This is the entrance to Hanu's Adventure, a game in the Dreamscape. You must transform yourself into a miniature Brother Hanu first to proceed.

t_ch: 被石头老板变小以后，爬楼梯就像攀登山峰一样困难，还是找找别的路吧。
t_en: After being shrunk by Boss Stone, climbing the staircase becomes as challenging as a mountain hike. Let's try another route.

t_ch: 石头老板放置的气球挡住了哈努兄弟的去路，没有被变小的伙伴或许能破坏它。
t_en: The balloons placed by Boss Stone have hindered Hanu's way. Those who are not shrunken can probably destroy the balloons.

t_ch: 哈努兄弟用智慧击败了石头老板的爪牙，这真的太酷了。
t_en: Hanu has overcome Boss Stone's minions with his wits. Impressive!

t_ch: 你得到了石头老板夺走的财宝，还有《小小哈努行动》的限量版故事书！
t_en: You receive the treasure looted by Boss Stone and the limited-edition storybook of Hanu's Adventure!

t_ch: 一阵奇怪的风吹过，在你的手里留下了一张幻造的书页。那转瞬化作电子数据的书页上，似乎记载了一首叙说洪荒时代的诗歌……
t_en: A strange wind blows past, leaving an imagenated page in your hands. The page, which instantly transforms into electronic data, seems to contain a poem that tells tales of the primordial age...

t_ch: 相逢就是缘。有什么我能够帮到你的吗？
t_en: Every encounter is unique and destined. How may I help you today?
I'm here for the Floating Grease Chronicles...

t_ch: 啊…原来是球棒浣熊的原型阁下。抱歉，我脸盲有点严重，还在像对陌生人一样打招呼。咳咳，是时候切换到「查令店长」的人格形态了。
t_en: Ah... So you're the prototype of Baseball Raccoon. Sorry, I have face blindness, thus still greeting you like a stranger. Ahem, it's time to switch to the Charing bookstore owner's persona.

t_ch: 重新自我介绍一下：在下是查令旧书店的店长，海伦·查令。而阁下身边的那本巨大立体书，就是历任店长的特殊朋友，《浮脂记事》，一本不会说话的幻造种古籍。
t_en: Allow me to reintroduce myself: I am Helen Charing, proprietor of Charing's Used Books. And that massive tome floating beside you is the Floating Grease Chronicles, an ancient imagenated text that can't speak, a special friend to all previous store owners.

t_ch: 俗话说「百闻不如一见」，阁下可以先确认一下《浮脂记事》被稍稍修复的状态。
t_en: As they say, "Seeing is believing." Why don't you check out the slightly reinstated state of the Floating Grease Chronicles yourself?

t_ch: 阁下或许听说过：{RUBY_B#阿哈}乐子神{RUBY_E#}曾在这个世界流下了一滴眼泪，这片土地因此而变得与众不同。
t_en: Perhaps you've heard the tale: {RUBY_B#Aha}The Laughter{RUBY_E#} once shed a tear in this world, and that's what made this land so extraordinary.

t_ch: 在那个时候，这里不仅不是{RUBY_B#公司的领地}二相乐园{RUBY_E#}，甚至也不能说是{RUBY_B#绘师的国度}古弁才天国{RUBY_E#}。《浮脂记事》便是关于那个时代的传说歌谣。
t_en: Back then, this place was not only not {RUBY_B#IPC's Domain}Planarcadia{RUBY_E#}, but it couldn't even be called {RUBY_B#Realm of the Artists}ancient Benzaitengoku{RUBY_E#}. The Floating Grease Chronicles tells the legends of that era.

t_ch: 历史辗转，有人将那些传说歌谣绘制成了幻造种的形式，分为《兔》《猴》《狐》《蛙》等篇目——也就是阁下见到的这些动物形象。
t_en: As history rolled on, someone turned those legendary ballads into imagenae forms, divided into chapters like Rabbit, Monkey, Fox, Frog, which are the animal figures you see before you now.

t_ch: 据说每一种动物都象征着彼时生活习惯各不相同的人们…所以它们也各有各的性格。
t_en: They say each animal represents people with their own distinct way of life back then, so each of them has their own unique personality.

t_ch: 可惜，年悠月久，失去愿力的书页就像是被抽去书钉和缝线的书籍，很容易散架。那些小动物们也纷纷跑得不知所踪了。
t_en: Sadly, as years and months went by, the pages that lost their Wishpower became like books with their staples and bindings removed, falling apart easily. Those little animals scattered and disappeared without a trace.

t_ch: 唉，可叹呐，歌谣被悲悼伶人们挪用、改编，又被假面愚者们进一步拿来揶揄。人们早忘了原典，却把变形后的故事奉为正朔。
t_en: Alas, what a pity... The ballad was appropriated and adapted by the Mourning Actors, then further mocked by the Masked Fools. People have long forgotten the original text, yet they revere these distorted stories as gospel.

t_ch: 不过，身为一名旧书店店长，在下也相信另一件事：只有流通的知识才是真正有用的知识。阁下已经为《浮脂记事》追回了一页残片…有没有兴趣帮我继续寻索？
t_en: However, as the proprietor of an antiquarian bookstore, I believe in something else: knowledge is only truly useful when it's shared. You've already recovered one fragment of the Floating Grease Chronicles... Would you be interested in helping me search for more?

t_ch: 我喜欢发掘隐秘的历史。
t_en: I love uncovering hidden history.

t_ch: 好极了，爱书之人总会不期而遇。有您的帮助，《浮脂记事》总会再度重聚一处。
t_en: Perfect! Book lovers always find each other by chance. With your help, the Floating Grease Chronicles will be reunited once again.

t_ch: 有什么更实际的好处吗？
t_en: Any more practical benefits?

t_ch: 收集《浮脂记事》的过程中，您可以在画中世界收获到对星际旅行有用的珍贵道具。
t_en: While collecting fragments of the Floating Grease Chronicles, you can obtain valuable items useful for space travel in the World in Canvas.
Why is it called Floating Grease Chronicles?

t_ch: 啊。「浮脂」是古弁才天国人对于混沌初开状态的一种比喻——世间万物就像水面上漂浮的油脂一样，关系不明、居无定所。
t_en: Ah, "Floating Grease" was an ancient metaphor for the primordial chaos, where everything in the world was like oil floating on water, with unclear relationships and no fixed place.

t_ch: 也像萍水相逢的人们…因为没有刻意建构的命运，反而会产生出各种各样的奇妙的化学反应。
t_en: Just like strangers crossing paths... When there's no deliberately constructed destiny, all sorts of wonderful chemistry can spark between them.
Leave it to me.

t_ch: 咳咳。既然事情已经讲明，我也就不用维持终日笑脸迎人的店长人设啦。
t_en: Ahem. Now that everything's out in the open, I can finally drop this whole "cheerful shopkeeper who smiles all day" act.

t_ch: 总之，这件事就拜托您了。就像之前说的，《浮脂记事》的《兔》《猴》《狐》《蛙》等等篇目有自己的人格，它们的碎片逃到了各处。
t_en: Anyway, I'll leave this matter in your hands. As mentioned before, the Rabbit, Monkey, Fox, Frog, and other chapters from the Floating Grease Chronicles have their own personalities, and their fragments have escaped to various places.

t_ch: 将这些由书页拟态而来的动物从被束缚、分解的现状中解放出来，就可以还原出一段湮没在时光中的原始记载，了解到这颗星球过往的传说歌谣。
t_en: By liberating these animals, born from the mimicry of pages, from their bound and fragmented state, you can restore a piece of primordial record lost to time, uncovering the legendary songs and tales of this planet.
Leave it to me.

t_ch: 太感谢了。说不定，有朝一日我们还能发现{RUBY_B#阿哈}乐子神{RUBY_E#}的秘密呢！
t_en: Thank you so much. Maybe one day we'll discover the secret of {RUBY_B#Aha}The Laughter{RUBY_E#}!
Don't get your hopes up.

t_ch: 没关系，尽力而为就是最好的！说不定，有朝一日我们还能发现{RUBY_B#阿哈}乐子神{RUBY_E#}的秘密呢？
t_en: Don't worry, just do your best! One day we'll even discover the secret of {RUBY_B#Aha}The Laughter{RUBY_E#}, perhaps?

t_ch: 用惯了移动电子设备的你鲜少与这样古典又笨重的通讯装置打交道。不过，很明显地，如果你现在一定想要用一用电话亭，那么时机已经完全成熟了。
t_en: You rarely deal with such old and bulky communication devices, though it's obvious you'll get a chance to find out how phone booths work now.

t_ch: 你瞧——这电话亭中空无一人，附近似乎也没有急于通信的贝洛伯格居民。这无疑是个独占电话亭的好时机。
t_en: Nobody in the phone booth... No Belobogians dying to use it, either. A good time to hog it all to yourself.
All right, I got to check it out.

t_ch: 哈，就知道你会这么说。尽管在这小小的一方天地中一探究竟吧。
t_en: Heh, I knew you'd say that. Let's explore this small place a bit.
Maybe next time.

t_ch: 当然可以。电话亭就在这里，哪儿也不会去。
t_en: Sure. The phone booth isn't going anywhere.

t_ch: 细心的你在漆皮有些剥落的转轮键盘电话机边发现了一张小卡片。「正当权益会社」，后附了一串数字——大概是电话号码，你想。
t_en: You spot a small card next to the rotary dial telephone with peeling paint. It reads "The Rightful Rights Society" followed by what you think is a phone number.

t_ch: 6…8…7…7…你的手指开始自发地拨动转盘上的数字。噢，这下糟了——假如这电话真能拨通，你就真得开始考虑如何打招呼了。
t_en: 6...8...7...7...You start dialing the number. Oops, now you got to figure out what to say if it does connect.

t_ch: 一声脆响，你意识到电话那头的人已经拿起了听筒，准备与你进行一轮激烈的言语交锋。箭在弦上——开始你的即兴表演吧！
t_en: You realize the call is picked up on the other end, and that person is prepared to engage in a heated exchange of words with you. Time for improv!

t_ch: 「正当权益会社」。有什么能帮到您吗？
t_en: The Rightful Rights Society! How may we help you?
Ah, yes!
What can we do for you?
Excuse me, I got the wrong number.

t_ch: 好的，祝您度过愉快的一天。
t_en: All right, have a nice day.

t_ch: 短暂的交锋以你率先投降告终。逃避可耻，但有用——至少你不用再承受与陌生人谈天带来的巨大压力了。
t_en: You give up quickly. Embarrassing, yes, but at least you don't have to worry about talking to a stranger.

t_ch: 我想了解一下你们这个…会社。
t_en: I'd like to know more about this... Society of yours.

t_ch: 啊，当然可以！我们「正当权益会社」是许多贝洛伯格人自发组建的组织——专注于帮助所有会员暂时逃离家庭和责任的束缚！
t_en: Why, of course! We are a volunteer group consisting of Belobogians that help all members temporarily escape the bond of families and responsibilities!

t_ch: 我们「正当权益会社」本质上是一个公益组织。入会不需要缴纳任何费用，只要在您有空时配合协助其他成员即可。
t_en: We are essentially a nonprofit organization with no fees to join. You just have to help fellow members in your spare time.

t_ch: 您也可以抢先体验我们的服务——本社为有入社意向的候选人提供一次免费试用机会。只要通过电话提交您的需求，我们即刻安排！
t_en: We also offer a one-time trial service for free. Just call us and let us know what you need, and we'll arrange it for you!

t_ch: 好像又没了，先聊到这里吧。
t_en: Okay, I'll think about it.

t_ch: 好的，祝您度过愉快的一天。
t_en: All right, have a nice day.

t_ch: 你实在想不到这场对话还能如何延续下去，于是你选择了高举白旗投降。希望你不会消耗太多时间来遗忘这场简短又尴尬的谈话。
t_en: You have no idea what more to say, so you ended the call. Try not to dwell on this brief yet awkward conversation.
I got something you can help with!

t_ch: 言尽于此，是时候开始真正的表演了！你开始讲述自己堪称传奇的人生轨迹——
t_en: Let's get the show started with your own legendary life story...

t_ch: 你是贝洛伯格某望族旁系第二十三代正统继承人；你天生丽质——秀发如彩霞，挥泪即珍珠；你家境殷实——卧榻逾半亩，出恭须驾车……
t_en: You are the <unbreak>23</unbreak>rd generation and rightful heir of a certain famous Belobog family. You are beautiful — your hair spreads as rosy clouds and even the tears you cry seem like pearls. You are wealthy — you're waited on hand and foot and given the best in life...
Oh my! How impressive!

t_ch: 然而，像你这种{F#富家千金}{M#富家阔少}本该自甘堕落，但你没有——你反其道而行之，将有限的精力投入到无限的自我提升中……
t_en: Yet, not only did a {F#rich girl}{M#rich fop} like you avoid becoming spoiled, you even dedicated yourself to self-improvement...

t_ch: 不仅如此，你还在黑夜里化身义士，四处打击犯罪、劫富济贫，甚至还受过大守护者亲自接见。只可惜，这一切的一切，都非你的本愿……
t_en: More still, you fought crimes, helped the poor, and even met the Supreme Guardian. However, that's not your true aspiration...
The Supreme Guardian?

t_ch: 其实，你最大的夙愿不过是摆脱生活中的三两琐事，再前往「机械屋」亲眼看看你的偶像——希露瓦！如今万事俱备，只欠「正当权益会社」拉一把了！
t_en: Your greatest wish is just to get rid of the chores in life and go see your idol Serval at the workshop again! You just need a little help from the Rightful Rights Society to do that.

t_ch: 你将自己的需求和盘托出。你很清楚，自己波澜壮阔的一生无疑打动了电话那边的接线员，因为你分明听到那人隐隐的抽泣声。
t_en: You've explained your needs in detail. It's clear your awesome story touched whoever took the call, as indicated by the faint sobbing.

t_ch: 谈话的最后，对方连连答应你的各种无理需求，甚至还安慰你不必为难以实现梦想而暗自消沉。那人是这样说的——
t_en: In the end, they yielded to each of your unreasonable requests, even encouraging you not to let the impossible dreams keep you down. Their response is as follows...

t_ch: 您放心！我们「正当权益会社」向来使命必达！
t_en: Don't worry. The Rightful Rights Society always comes through!

t_ch: 在惺惺相惜的氛围中，你与对方道别，而对方迅速挂断了对话。毫无疑问，这场比试是你获胜了。
t_en: You say your goodbye and the other end quickly hangs up. Undoubtedly, you've won this round.

t_ch: 好吧，算了，我确实没兴趣。
t_en: Never mind. I'm not interested.

t_ch: 好的，祝您度过愉快的一天。
t_en: All right, have a nice day.

t_ch: 浅浅试探了几轮对方的话术，你便对这场比试失去了兴趣。对方谈及的内容确实并不能引起你的太多关注，还是别把宝贵的时间浪费在这种无益的谈话上了。
t_en: You lose interest in the competition after a few rounds of banter. The spiel you get isn't scintillating enough to waste your time on.
Mysterious Kid

t_ch: 嘿…你是来参加「黑暗拳斗大赛」的吗？
t_en: Hey, are you here for the Dark Fist Tournament?
What is the Dark Fist Tournament?

t_ch: 如果你没拿到俱乐部里的邀请函，我可不能多说……
t_en: I can't disclose much if you don't have the club invitation...

t_ch: 我只能说，「黑暗拳斗大赛」只有被选中的战士才能受邀参加。这可是货真价实的拳头比拼……
t_en: All I can say is that only selected fighters get to enter the high-class fisticuff known as the Dark Fist Tournament.

t_ch: …比俱乐部里的表演要刺激一万倍！
t_en: ...It's a thousand times more exciting than anything else in the club!

t_ch: （看来，这搏击俱乐部里有个所谓的邀请函……）
t_en: (I guess you need an invitation from the Fight Club...)
I'm not interested in brawling...
...Then go away!
(What's wrong with this kid?)
Investigate

t_ch: 下层区的搏击俱乐部只有两种状态——人声鼎沸，以及更加人声鼎沸。
t_en: The Fight Club in the Underworld comes in two ambiances: Noisy, and noisier.

t_ch: 但就在这样一个喧哗热闹的场所里，你在角落中发现了与周遭环境格格不入的那一位。
t_en: But in such a boisterous venue, you notice someone conspicuously out of place.

t_ch: 善于观察生活的你心里明白，每个欢闹的场合中总会有这样一位不苟言笑的家伙。但是，眼前的它似乎有点与众不同……
t_en: You know from past observations that there would be one such person in every such occasion, but this one seems different somehow...

t_ch: 这古朴的信封看起来毫不起眼，只静静地躺在被用作茶几边桌的木质板条箱上，散发着其独有的气质——
t_en: The quaint envelope sits unremarkably on a wooden crate that is being used as a side table, yet it exudes a unique quality of its own...

t_ch: 沉默、优雅、遗世独立，甚至有点…黑暗。除此之外，你还看到封面上写着几个歪歪扭扭的大字「邀请函」。
t_en: Silent, elegant, independent, even a hint of... darkness. You also see "Invitation" written in big, crooked letters.

t_ch: 你的视线已经几乎无法从它身上移开了。
t_en: You find yourself unable to pry your eyes from it.
It's mine now!

t_ch: 正确的决定。没人能拒绝如此沉默、优雅、遗世独立，甚至还带点黑暗的邀请函。
t_en: That's the right move. No one could resist such a silent, elegant, independent, and slightly "dark" envelope of invitation.
Mysterious Kid

t_ch: 嘿…你是来参加「黑暗拳斗大赛」的吗？
t_en: Hey, are you here for the Dark Fist Tournament?
What is the Dark Fist Tournament?

t_ch: 如果你没拿到俱乐部里的邀请函，我可不能多说……
t_en: I can't disclose much if you don't have the club invitation...

t_ch: 我只能说，「黑暗拳斗大赛」只有被选中的战士才能受邀参加。这可是货真价实的拳头比拼……
t_en: All I can say is that only selected fighters get to enter the high-class fisticuff known as the Dark Fist Tournament.

t_ch: …比俱乐部里的表演要刺激一万倍！
t_en: ...It's a thousand times more exciting than anything else in the club!

t_ch: （看来，这搏击俱乐部里有个所谓的邀请函……）
t_en: (I guess you need an invitation from the Fight Club...)
See, I got the invitation.

t_ch: …没想到你竟然收到了我们的邀请函。
t_en: ...I didn't expect you to get an invitation.

t_ch: 我承认了，你是被选中的战士…跟我来吧。
t_en: I see you're a chosen one. Follow me.
I'm not interested in brawling...
...Then go away!
(What's wrong with this kid?)
Mysterious Kid

t_ch: 各位被选中的战士啊，我们的「黑暗拳斗大赛地下擂台」终于在今天迎来了新的挑战者……
t_en: Attention, chosen ones, the Underground Ring's Dark Fist Tournament finally has a new challenger...
Please welcome {NICKNAME}!
Yeah, a newcomer!
Looks pathetic to me...

t_ch: 你这小孩子怎么说话呢？！
t_en: Why would you say that, kid?
Because you do look pathetic!
Isn't this supposed to be an "underground" fighting ring?

t_ch: 嘁，下层区当然在地下了！
t_en: Of course it is! Where could the Underworld be but underground?

t_ch: 我看你是什么都不懂喔——小心，别输到哭着鼻子回家！
t_en: I see you know nothing. Just don't bawl your eyes out when you lose!

t_ch: 小朋友玩这个有点危险吧…
t_en: Isn't this dangerous for children?

t_ch: 哼，你怕了？怕了就赶紧回家吧！
t_en: Are you chickening out? You wanna go home now?

t_ch: 肃静，肃静！{NICKNAME}初来乍到，先让本主持人介绍一下参赛选手和规则。
t_en: Quiet, quiet! It's {NICKNAME}'s first time, so let me introduce the competitors and the rules.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，看好了！在你面前的这三位，是我们地下擂台的「四大天王」——
t_en: {NICKNAME}, the three people in front of you are the "Formidable Four."

t_ch: 只要你能在一局一胜制的「猜拳」比赛中将他们全部击败，就能得到我们「黑暗拳斗大赛」组委会准备的超豪华奖励！
t_en: If you can beat them all in single rounds of rock-paper-scissors, you'll win a fabulous prize from the committee!
Wait, so the "brawl" is just rock-paper-scissors?
Why don't you ever say anything?

t_ch: 高手都不说话，他是我们「四大天王」里最高的高手！
t_en: Silent waters run deep, and he's the best of the Formidable Four!
Uh, okay.
Why are there only three people in the Formidable Four?

t_ch: 吉米正被他爸爸关在家里写作业呢，没办法出来玩……
t_en: Jimmy's dad won't let him out until he's done with his homework...

t_ch: 嘘——「四大天王」的动向可是最高机密，不许随意外传！
t_en: Hush, the whereabouts of the Formidable Four are top secret!
There's a committee? So formal.

t_ch: 当然了，我们这是正规比赛！
t_en: Of course, we run officially sanctioned matches!
We run officially sanctioned matches!
Are you a parrot?

t_ch: 你还敢羞辱我！看我怎么收拾你！
t_en: You dare make fun of me? I'll get you for that!
Uh, okay.

t_ch: 好了，废话不多说——让比赛开始吧！
t_en: All right, quit yapping and start the match!
Mysterious Kid

t_ch: 快去挑战三位「四大天王」吧——记住，是一局一胜！
t_en: Go challenge the Formidable Four. Remember, it's one round each!
First of the Formidable Four

t_ch: 你好呀，{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，要来玩猜拳吗？
t_en: Hi {F#miss}{M#mister}, here for rock-paper-scissors?

t_ch: 哦，我们的新秀{NICKNAME}即将对玛丽发起挑战！
t_en: The rookie {NICKNAME} is taking on Mary!
On my signal... One, two...

t_ch: 战斗来得总是如此突然。所以，你准备出什么？
t_en: Battles can happen unexpectedly. So, what's your move?
Scissors!
Paper!

t_ch: 什么？！玛丽竟然被{NICKNAME}击败了…！
t_en: Oh no, {NICKNAME} has beaten Mary...!
Rock!
Scissors!

t_ch: 什么？！玛丽竟然被{NICKNAME}击败了…！
t_en: Oh no, {NICKNAME} has beaten Mary...!
Paper!
Rock!

t_ch: 什么？！玛丽竟然被{NICKNAME}击败了…！
t_en: Oh no, {NICKNAME} has beaten Mary...!

t_ch: 身为「四大天王」之一的玛丽竟然遗憾落败…这就是「黑暗拳斗大赛」新秀的实力吗！
t_en: Mary, one of the Formidable Four, has lost... Do we have a rising star in the Dark Fist Tournament?
I impressed myself!
...That's it?

t_ch: 当然！刚才都说了，我们这是一局一胜制的！
t_en: Yeah. Didn't I say it's single-round only?

t_ch: 噢，我输啦！{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}太厉害了！
t_en: Oh, I lost! {F#Miss}{M#Mister} is just too good!

t_ch: （看来这孩子只是在单纯地享受猜拳游戏的快乐……）
t_en: (Looks like this child is simply having fun...)
Say your line, Mary!
Huh?
The one about the weakest...
Oh, right!

t_ch: {F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，虽然你赢了，但其实我是「四大天王」里最弱的那个！你可不要得意忘形哦！
t_en: You may have won, {F#Miss}{M#Mister}, but I'm the weakest of the Formidable Four, so enjoy the victory while you can!
First of the Formidable Four
{F#Miss}{M#Mister} is just too good!
Second of the Formidable Four

t_ch: 哦，我们的新秀{NICKNAME}即将对汤姆发起挑战！
t_en: The rookie {NICKNAME} is taking on Tom!
Are you ready?
Not talking because silent waters run deep?

t_ch: 看来，我们的汤姆选手已经准备好了！那么，听我口令！三、二——
t_en: It looks like Tom is ready. On my signal... One, two...

t_ch: 这个沉默不语的孩子对你们一大一小两人说的话没有丝毫反应。你无法确认他是否真的已经准备好接受你的挑战。
t_en: The silent kid doesn't react to anything that is said at all. You're not sure if he is indeed ready for you.

t_ch: 就算假设这个问题的答案是肯定的，你仍无法从他微小的动作或表情中判断出他可能出什么——因为他确实没有任何反应。
t_en: Even if he was, you still couldn't get any read off him, because he showed no tell.

t_ch: 不得不说，高手总是滴水不漏的。所以，你准备出什么？
t_en: Gotta say, aces are airtight for a reason. So, what's your move?
Scissors!

t_ch: 什么？！汤姆竟然出了石头！
t_en: What? Tom chose rock!
Rock!
What? Tom chose paper!
Paper!

t_ch: 什么？！汤姆竟然出了剪刀！
t_en: What? Tom chose scissors!

t_ch: 眼前的孩子依旧纹丝不动。怎么会有人——我是说，小孩——竟然能对自己获得的胜利无动于衷？你实在想不明白。
t_en: The kid is still not reacting. How can anyone, let alone a child, not respond to winning? You just can't fathom it at all.

t_ch: 但你很清楚一件事：你输了，而且输得心服口服。
t_en: But you know one thing: You lost, and decisively.
Second of the Formidable Four
Third of the Formidable Four

t_ch: 哈，小小{NICKNAME}，就让我戴维来会会你！
t_en: It's time for you, {NICKNAME}, to take on me, David!

t_ch: 这个目中无人的小子已经高高举起他的右手，正准备如他自己所言那般…「会会你」。
t_en: The haughty kid raises his right hand, wanting a piece of you.

t_ch: 你大概已经看这个狂妄的小孩不爽很久了。现在，你的心里恐怕正想着自己非得要挫挫他的锐气才叫痛快，而且一定要不择手段才行。
t_en: This kid is already getting on your nerves, and now you're itching to beat him... thoroughly.

t_ch: 可这所谓的「黑暗拳斗大赛」不过是小孩子们过家家、不过是一场幼稚的童年游戏…你真的有必要如此上纲上线吗？他还只是个孩子啊！
t_en: But the so-called Dark Fist Tournament is just a game and he's just a kid. Do you really want to rub it in his face?

t_ch: 在你决定出什么之前，希望你能把这个问题仔细考虑清楚。
t_en: Before you decide, think it through.
Scissors!

t_ch: 是啊，何必和小孩子置气？这可不是你的作风。于是，你决定遵守游戏规则，同这位自大的小朋友公平对决——
t_en: Picking on kids is not your style at all. Therefore, you decide to follow the rules and play fair...
What, you're choosing scissors?

t_ch: 在好运的加持下，你终于给了这骄傲的小朋友一记深刻的教训。或者，用简单的话来说——你赢了！
t_en: With luck on your side, you taught the arrogant kid a lesson. Or in other words, you won!
Rock!

t_ch: 是啊，何必和小孩子置气？这可不是你的作风。于是，你决定遵守游戏规则，同这位自大的小朋友公平对决——
t_en: Picking on kids is not your style at all. Therefore, you decide to follow the rules and play fair...
What, you're choosing rock?

t_ch: 在好运的加持下，你终于给了这骄傲的小朋友一记深刻的教训。或者，用简单的话来说——你赢了！
t_en: With luck on your side, you taught the arrogant kid a lesson. Or in other words, you won!
Paper!

t_ch: 是啊，何必和小孩子置气？这可不是你的作风。于是，你决定遵守游戏规则，同这位自大的小朋友公平对决——
t_en: Picking on kids is not your style at all. Therefore, you decide to follow the rules and play fair...
What, you're choosing paper?

t_ch: 在好运的加持下，你终于给了这骄傲的小朋友一记深刻的教训。或者，用简单的话来说——你赢了！
t_en: With luck on your side, you taught the arrogant kid a lesson. Or in other words, you won!
I have to put him in his place!

t_ch: 哈，这个想法可真是太邪恶了——但崇高道德似乎对此表示并不在意……
t_en: How evil! But the higher powers don't seem bothered by it...

t_ch: 换句话说，你的这个念头或许没有想象中那么邪恶。倘若再深入思考，恐怕好好教育教育这个小孩子、教会他何谓「以牙还牙」才是正确的哩！
t_en: In other words, it actually might not be a bad idea to humble the kid with "an eye for an eye."

t_ch: 没多久，你便将自己彻底说服了。那就放手去做吧！至少那司掌「巡猎」命途的岚听过此事后一定会很高兴的。
t_en: In no time you've convinced yourself to teach the kid a lesson. So just do it! At the very least Lan, who oversees the Path of The Hunt, would be glad to hear this.

t_ch: 先随便出一个，然后再换手！
t_en: Distract and switch!

t_ch: 精彩的计谋！在裁判一声令下、你们二人出手的瞬间，你便高声叫道「快看，娜塔莎大夫来了！」——
t_en: Brilliant plan! As you two reveal your hands, you shout, "Look, is that Dr. Natasha!?"

t_ch: 那涉世未深的「四大天王」哪里见过这样的奇计！他果然被你一声惊呼吓得魂不守舍。你抓住这个机会，迅速将手势从石头变成了必胜的剪刀。
t_en: Your young and naive opponents have never seen such a tactic before! He indeed is startled by your words, and you grasp the opportunity to change your rock into the winning scissors.

t_ch: 没有人发现你的小动作。你赢了——而且赢得很痛快。
t_en: No one notices the move. You win, and in much delight.
Third of the Formidable Four
No! How did I lose?
Mysterious Kid

t_ch: 没想到，新秀{NICKNAME}竟然拿下两分！看来赢家已经诞生，游戏结束了……
t_en: The rookie {NICKNAME} has scored two points! I guess we have a winner and it's game over...

t_ch: 按照我们「黑暗拳斗大赛」的规矩，你理应得到奖励！
t_en: According to the rules, you get a prize!

t_ch: 来，这是我们精心准备的——「愤怒拳套」——的配方！
t_en: It's the secret to making our Wrathful Gauntlets!

t_ch: 你只要按照这个方法来做，就能打造出威力巨大的拳套啦！怎么样，是不是很「黑暗拳斗」啊？
t_en: Just follow its instructions and you can create powerful gauntlets! How's that for a Dark Fist!?
Dark, indeed!

t_ch: 你们哪里搞到这种危险玩意的…
t_en: Where did you get such a dangerous thing?

t_ch: 这是我们「黑暗拳斗大赛地下擂台」的秘密！
t_en: That's an Underground Ring Dark Fist Tournament secret!

t_ch: 好了，时候不早了——玛丽，汤姆，戴维，我们回诊所吧！
t_en: Okay, it's getting late. Mary, Tom, and David, we should get back to the clinic.
All right. Let's go!

t_ch: 你等着，我一定会回来的！
t_en: I'll be back. Just you wait!
Gatekeeper of the Underground Ring
Master of the Dark Fist
First of the Formidable Four
Second of the Formidable Four
Third of the Formidable Four
